methodsAttempted.query: site:torontomu.ca artificial intelligence generative AI academic integrity teaching guidance Toronto Metropolitan University
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official TMU teaching guidance, Academic Integrity Office AI FAQs, a TMU Library guide, graduate guidance, and a research integrity PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: site:torontomu.ca generative AI privacy security procurement approved tools Toronto Metropolitan University
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found mostly research reports and event/course pages; the central Google AI Tools Help Centre was discovered through the teaching guidance resource links.
Official TMU GenAI teaching guidance links to the Academic Integrity AI FAQ and Google AI Tools Help Centre; direct HTTP fetches verified all three pages.
Checked robots.txt and found no disallow rule covering the three HTML source pages used for evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery located three verified TMU pages with substantive university guidance on GenAI in learning and teaching, academic integrity, and TMU-provided Google AI tools. Research reports and news items were documented as rejected because they did not provide university policy or operational guidance for student or employee use of AI.

targets.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

stopConditions: Stop after official TMU pages support direct claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide binding rules from news, events, research reports, or external Google Docs.
Keep source snippets short and do not stage raw HTML, PDFs, screenshots, or browser profiles.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.claimText: TMU guidance states that, unless a course instructor explicitly communicates otherwise, GenAI use for coursework is not permitted.

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unless explicitly communicated by the course instructor, the use of GenAI for coursework is not permitted

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.claimText: TMU's Academic Integrity Office says students should assume that using AI to complete assessments is prohibited unless the instructor explicitly states otherwise.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unless explicitly stated by the instructor, students should assume that using AI to complete assessments is prohibited.

artifacts.claimText: TMU's Academic Integrity Office identifies submitting work created in whole or in part by AI tools, unless expressly permitted by the faculty member or contract lecturer, as a Policy 60 misrepresentation issue.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 5.5. submitting work created in whole or in part by artificial intelligence tools unless expressly permitted by the Faculty/Contract Lecturer;
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Does Policy 60 prohibit student use of AI for assessments?

artifacts.claimText: TMU's GenAI learning and teaching guidance tells users not to submit anyone's personal information into GenAI models without consent or an ability to opt out.

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Do not submit anyone’s personal information into GenAI models without consent or ability to opt out and be aware that, in many cases, material uploaded or entered into GenAI tools is used to train future models.

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.claimText: TMU says the Gemini App and NotebookLM are available to TMU employees and students and that using a TMU account means activities will not be used to train Google's AI models.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Both tools are available to TMU employees and students... By using your TMU account, you can ensure that your data will not be used to train Google's AI models.

artifacts.claimText: TMU's Google AI Tools Help Centre says only public or low-sensitivity information should be entered into TMU's Google AI tools, and sensitive or confidential information should not be used in prompts, uploads, or activities.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Only public or low-sensitivity information should be input to these tools. Sensitive and confidential information should never be used in prompts, uploaded files or other activities.

artifacts.claimText: TMU states that privacy and AI impact assessments and a security risk assessment were conducted before releasing the Gemini App and NotebookLM at TMU.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Prior to the release of the Gemini App and NotebookLM at TMU, we conducted privacy and AI impact assessments... and a security risk assessment with the Office of the Chief Information Security Officer.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: How does Google protect my data?

artifacts.claimText: TMU guidance says AI detectors are not currently endorsed and directs instructors with GenAI misuse concerns to the Academic Integrity Office for investigation recommendations.

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI detectors are not currently endorsed by TMU. If you have concerns about unauthorized use of GenAI by students, contact the Academic Integrity Office for recommendations on how to investigate the concern.

citation.citationTitle: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Learning and Teaching at TMU

artifacts.title: Toronto Metropolitan University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found official TMU guidance on GenAI in learning and teaching, academic integrity expectations for AI in assessments, and responsible use of TMU-provided Google AI tools. Claims are limited to official TMU pages and remain staged, non-canonical, and agent-reviewed.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
External Google Docs, third-party resources, official news items, and research reports were not used as claim evidence.
No login-walled, CAPTCHA-protected, paywalled, or robots-disallowed material was accessed.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
